citations_found.summary: Legal Document View ] \ , Cited by \] Rajasthan High Court - Jaipur The State Of Rajasthan vs Motiram on 13 February, 1973 Equivalent citations: AIR1973RAJ223, 1973 WLN132 ``` JUDGMENT Sohan Nath Modi, J. ``` 1\. These two appeals are directed against the two separate judgments and decrees of the District Judge. Kotah, dated 31-5-69. As common questions of law and facts are involved in these appeals, they are being disposed of together. 2\. The plaintiff-respondent instituted two suits in the Co...
citations_found.ai_summary:
Here's a 2-3 sentence summary of the case:

The dispute in the case of The State Of Rajasthan vs Motiram was about the plaintiff's claim for additional payment for work done on a drainage syphon project, which he argued was increased due to changes in the scope of work and new items added. The court ultimately decided that the plaintiff was not entitled to the additional payment of Rs. 7804/-, as the changes in the scope of work and new items were not agreed upon by the parties in the original tender agreement. The court's decision was based on the fact that the plaintiff had already been paid for the work done as per the rates mentioned in the tender.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here's a 2-3 sentence summary of the case:

The dispute in the case of The State Of Rajasthan vs Motiram was about the payment to be made to the plaintiff, Motiram, for constructing a drainage syphon at a canal, where the quantities of work increased and new items of work were added, resulting in a higher cost. The plaintiff claimed an additional amount of Rs. 7804, but this claim was rejected by the Chief Engineer. The Rajasthan High Court ultimately decided in favor of the plaintiff, allowing him to recover the additional amount claimed.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the case:

The dispute in the case of The State Of Rajasthan vs Motiram was about the plaintiff's claim for additional payment for work done on a drainage syphon project, which he argued was increased due to changes in the scope of work and additional items added to the project. The court ultimately decided that the plaintiff was not entitled to the additional payment he claimed, as the changes to the project were not significant enough to warrant the 40% increase in rates he sought.
